name: Dehesa Boyal and Valtravieso Reservoir Loop
name_original: Ruta de las Esculturas, Embalse de Valtravieso y Castillo de Magalia — circular desde Navas del Marqués
description: This difficult 12.7-mile touring cycling route from Navas del Marqués features an outdoor sculpture park, a reservoir, and a castle.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, pine forests, and open dehesa. The region features several reservoirs and mountain passes, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation changes are common, with routes often including significant climbs and descents. This area provides a mix of natural scenery and historical points of interest. Best touring cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués The most popular touring cycling route is Dehesa Boyal and Valtravieso Reservoir Loop, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the dehesa landscape and passes by the Valtravieso Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cruz Verde Pass – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Robledo de Chavela, a difficult 44.3 miles (71.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it navigates the mountain pass. Local touring cyclists also love the Viewpoint of the city of Madrid – Colmenar del Arroyo loop from Robledo de Chavela, a 64.5 miles (103.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Las Navas Del Marqués is defined by mountain passes, reservoirs, and extensive dehesa woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to advanced touring bike routes. The routes in Las Navas Del Marqués are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Navas Del Marqués's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are 4 easy touring cycling routes available. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: The region features rolling hills, extensive pine forests, and open dehesa landscapes. You can expect varied terrain, often including significant climbs and descents as routes navigate mountain passes and reservoir areas.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is known for its challenging routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40359742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the city of Madrid – Colmenar del Arroyo loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 64.5-mile (103.9 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10415947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha – Magalia Castle-Palace loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; covers 82.3 miles (132.4 km) with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can visit on a touring bike?
A: Many routes offer scenic views. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5682647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de las Bereas Viewpoint (View of the Valtravieso Reservoir)&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3005039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4219128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fraile Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, the region features several reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107436452/dehesa-boyal-and-valtravieso-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Boyal and Valtravieso Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores the area around the Valtravieso Reservoir. Other nearby bodies of water include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aceña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, especially in the midday sun, while winter can bring colder temperatures and occasional snow at higher elevations, particularly around mountain passes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Las Navas Del Marqués are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde Pass – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23842441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zarzalejo Village Center – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Robledo de Chavela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What should I consider bringing for a touring cycling trip in this region?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for elevation changes, it's advisable to bring appropriate gear for climbing and descending, sufficient water, snacks, and layers of clothing. A repair kit and navigation tools are also recommended, especially for longer or more remote routes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: The touring cycling routes in Las Navas Del Marqués are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the beauty of the dehesa woodlands, and the rewarding views.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Las Navas Del Marqués?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns and villages, such as Las Navas Del Marqués itself or Robledo de Chavela, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Specific parking details may vary by trailhead.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the touring cycling routes?
A: While some routes pass through or near villages where cafes and shops are available, many sections traverse natural, less populated areas. It's wise to plan your stops and carry enough provisions, especially on longer rides, as amenities might be sparse between towns.
tips: By bicycle from Navas del Marqués, you can take the path to the left to discover ''The sculpture route'' located in the Dehesa Boyal. It is a tour to see nine sculptural works. The route runs through an area with little slope. Ideal for families. Near this point there is a fountain.
It is worth stopping for a moment to enjoy the incredible views offered by this Fuente de las Bereas viewpoint overlooking the Valtravieso Reservoir.

Magalia Castle is a 16th century construction located in the town of Navas del Marqués that is worth visiting. It offers magnificent views of the town. Check schedules for guided views with the City Council.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring cycling route is approximately 20.5 km long with an elevation gain of 523 meters. It generally takes around 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and is considered an 'expert' level bike tour. It requires very good physical fitness, and there are segments where you may even need to push your bike due to challenging terrain. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. Approximately 12.8 km is asphalt, 3.27 km is paved, and about 4.39 km consists of unpaved sections. The unpaved parts can be challenging for touring bikes not specifically equipped for rougher surfaces, contributing to the route's difficult rating.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points of interest. You'll experience the unique Sculpture Route of the Dehesa Boyal, an open-air art initiative with over 70 sculptures. It also passes by the scenic Valtravieso Reservoir and the historic Magalia Castle-Palace.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers magnificent views, particularly from the Fuente de las Bereas Viewpoint (View of the Valtravieso Reservoir). You'll also enjoy scenic vistas of Navas del Marqués and the surrounding mountainous, forested landscape of the Sierra de Malagón.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Navas del Marqués. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas are usually available near the Dehesa Boyal or other public spaces in Navas del Marqués.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location at over 1,300 meters altitude in a mountainous area, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is usually milder and more pleasant for cycling, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and snow.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this touring cycling route, especially for the Dehesa Boyal?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Dehesa Boyal or to cycle this route. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit, especially concerning any specific rules for cycling within natural areas.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural areas are generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists. Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will cycle along parts of the Camino de Cepeda, Camino del Kilómetro, Presa Nueva, and Camino de las Callejas.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, it's crucial to bring a touring bicycle suitable for both paved and unpaved sections. Essential items include plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Due to the potential need to push your bike, comfortable and sturdy footwear is also recommended.
